prompt:
You are adjudicating a SCOPED set of architecture decisions for a VS Code extension
("Work Explorer") Set 093 Session 2. Give DECISIVE rulings (pick one option each,
one-line rationale I record in the disposition), not open-ended options. This ships
today as TypeScript + a webview client.js + JSON-protocol changes + tests.

## Settled context (operator-confirmed — do NOT re-litigate)

The Work Explorer renders ONE WAI-ARIA tree dialect: module (aria-level 1) ->
persistent child nodes `Plan` and `Session sets` (level 2) -> status buckets (3)
-> session-set rows (4). Session 1 shipped the persistent semantic child nodes.
Three module KINDS exist:
  - `declared`: a docs/modules.yaml entry. Has slug + resolved planPath.
  - `fallback`: an observed `module:` stamp slug the manifest does NOT declare.
    planPath is null; carries an `undeclared-slug` warning; always >=1 set.
  - `pseudo`: the module of UNSTAMPED sets (labeled `Default` when sole,
    `Unassigned` when other groups coexist). planPath = docs/planning/project-plan.md
    (legacy root plan); slug is "".

Session 2's job (spec, operator-confirmed):
- A module-ROW inline ACTION STRIP (revealed on hover/focus), with four actions:
  `AI Plan` (copy a module-targeted plan-authoring prompt), `Import Plan` (file
  picker -> module's planPath), `Open Plan` (open the module's plan in an editor),
  `AI Sets` (copy a module-targeted session-set decomposition prompt).
- The SAME actions mirrored in a right-click context menu on the module row AND
  in the Command Palette. Amendment 1 (both panel critics): actions live on the
  ROW, never as button-bearing tree NODES — nested interactive treeitems break
  WAI-ARIA tree keyboard semantics. Child nodes stay purely semantic.
- Row/context invocations CARRY the module (imply it): NO QuickPick and NO
  auto-select notice fires on these paths. The existing palette commands
  (dabbler.importPlan, dabbler.generateSessionSetPrompt, dabbler.newModule) keep
  today's QuickPick behavior for keyboard-driven use.
- `Assign legacy sets to module...` affordance on the `Unassigned` module: pick a
  target module + sets; stamp `module: <slug>` into each chosen set's spec.md via a
  FORMAT-PRESERVING write (manifest-validated; NEVER writes `default`/pseudo;
  refusal leaves files untouched and reports — mirroring the S3 modules.yaml
  appender posture).

## Existing seam (the code I extend)

`pickModuleForAuthoring(root, ui): Promise<ModulePickOutcome>` reads docs/modules.yaml
itself and returns one of: `{kind:"none"}` (no/empty manifest -> repo-level flow),
`{kind:"invalid-manifest"}` (present-but-broken -> error + abort), `{kind:"auto",
entry}` (exactly one module, fires showInformationMessage notice), `{kind:"picked",
entry}` (>=2 -> showQuickPick), `{kind:"cancelled"}`. Today's three authoring
handlers (copySessionSetGenPrompt, copyPlanningPrompt, importPlanFromFile) all call
it and then act on `.entry` (null => repo-level). The webview posts typed messages
to the host; a COMMAND_ALLOWLIST guards webview->host executeCommand dispatch. Row
context menus today are host-side vscode.window.showQuickPick keyed by the set's
globally-unique NAME (never module). Module rows currently post no action messages.
The spec.md "Session Set Configuration" block is a ```yaml fenced block; readers
parse `module:` with a line regex. `module` is a GROUPING attribute, never identity.

## DECISIONS TO RULE

**D1 — Threading an explicit module target through `pickModuleForAuthoring`.**
Proposed: add an optional 3rd arg `opts?: { preselectedSlug?: string }`. When
`preselectedSlug` is set, the function resolves it against the manifest and returns
`{kind:"picked", entry}` with NO showQuickPick and NO showInformationMessage notice;
if the slug is NOT a declared module (fallback/removed/pseudo ""), it returns...
what? Rule the not-found behavior decisively: (a) `{kind:"none"}` (fall to repo-level
plan), (b) `{kind:"cancelled"}` (abort — a stale row), (c) a new
`{kind:"unknown-module"}` outcome the callers map to abort-with-error. Consider that
the webview snapshot the row was rendered from can go stale (module deleted from
modules.yaml between render and click). Also confirm: is extending the ONE shared
function the right move vs a separate `resolveModuleBySlug` helper?

**D2 — Pseudo and fallback module strips.** Which of the four actions does each
non-declared kind expose, and what target?
  - PSEUDO (`Default`/`Unassigned`): planPath is the legacy root plan; AI Sets has
    no module to stamp. Proposed: AI Plan / Import Plan / Open Plan target the
    repo-level plan (the existing no-module `{kind:"none"}` path), AI Sets copies
    the repo-level (module-less) decomposition prompt. I.e. the pseudo strip is the
    repo-level authoring flow with no stamp. Confirm or correct.
  - FALLBACK (undeclared slug, planPath null, not in manifest): there is no plan
    slot and no manifest entry to stamp/validate against. Proposed: the fallback
    row shows NO action strip (or a strip whose only affordance points at the
    remediation — declaring the slug in modules.yaml); the undeclared-slug warning
    owns remediation. Rule decisively: no strip, a reduced strip, or the full strip
    (and if reduced, exactly which actions and their targets). Fail-loud /
    never-hide-work is the tie-breaker.

**D3 — Webview action-strip -> host protocol + WAI-ARIA placement + allowlist.**
Proposed: a new typed webview->host message `{type:"moduleAction", action:
"ai-plan"|"import-plan"|"open-plan"|"ai-sets", moduleSlug, moduleKind}` (NOT routed
through executeCommand/COMMAND_ALLOWLIST — it carries a closed action enum + module
identity, like the existing gettingStartedAction channel, so the command allowlist
contract is untouched). The host narrows the untrusted action/kind, resolves the
module, and dispatches. Confirm this over reusing executeCommand. For ARIA: the
strip buttons must sit OUTSIDE the module treeitem's ACCESSIBLE NAME (which is
aria-labelledby -> the header). Proposed placement: the strip is a sibling of the
`.module-header` inside the module treeitem, its buttons `role="button"
tabindex=-1/0` (roving), reachable by keyboard, and the treeitem's aria-labelledby
continues to point only at the header so the buttons never pollute the name — the
exact pattern the existing clickable `.row-verification-marker` uses. Confirm this is
WAI-ARIA-tree-safe (interactive descendants that are NOT treeitems, excluded from the
name), or prescribe the correct alternative (e.g. a roving secondary tabstop /
aria-describedby). Also: how does the CONTEXT MENU on a module row reach the host —
extend the existing `showRowContextMenu` (today keyed by set name) to also accept a
module identity, or a new `showModuleContextMenu` message? Rule decisively.

**D4 — The spec.md `module:` stamp writer (assign-legacy flow).** This is the
riskiest new code (mutates operator spec files). Rule:
  (i) WHERE in the spec.md to insert `module: <slug>`: inside the "Session Set
      Configuration" ```yaml block. Placement within the block (top of block? after
      a specific key? end of block?) and how to be format-preserving (regex insert
      vs YAML round-trip — the S3 appender chose regex insert + parse-after guard to
      avoid destroying comments). Handle: block already has a `module:` line
      (overwrite vs refuse), no config block at all (refuse loud), block present but
      no yaml fence (refuse).
  (ii) The REFUSAL posture and atomicity across MULTIPLE selected sets: is the
      multi-set stamp all-or-nothing, or per-file best-effort with a report? (S3
      appender is single-file fail-loud; here N files are chosen at once.)
  (iii) A parse-after-write guard mirroring assertAppendedManifestParses: what must
      it assert (the block still parses; `module:` now equals the slug; nothing else
      changed)?
  (iv) Confirm the never-write-`default`/pseudo and manifest-validated-slug guards,
      and that a set already stamped to the SAME module is a no-op (not a refusal).

Keep every ruling tight and implementable. Where I proposed a design, either bless it
with the one-line rationale or correct it decisively.
content:
## D1 — Threading a module target through `pickModuleForAuthoring`

**Ruling: (c) new `{kind:"unknown-module"}` outcome; callers map it to abort-with-error. Extend the shared function with `opts?: {preselectedSlug?: string}` — do NOT add a separate helper.**
Rationale: a preselected slug that no longer resolves is a stale-snapshot race, and the only fail-loud answer is an explicit error ("module removed from modules.yaml — refresh"); reusing the one manifest-reading seam keeps invalid-manifest detection and entry shape single-sourced.

- Resolution order inside the function: read manifest → **broken ⇒ `invalid-manifest`** (a stale slug against a broken manifest is never "unknown", it's "invalid") → if `preselectedSlug` set: resolve → found ⇒ `{kind:"picked", entry}` with **no showQuickPick, no showInformationMessage**; not found ⇒ `{kind:"unknown-module"}` → else today's `none`/`auto`/`picked` logic unchanged.
- `unknown-module` and `invalid-manifest` get distinct operator messages (deleted-row vs corrupt-file); both abort, neither falls to repo-level.
- Reject (a)/(b): `none` silently misdirects a module-targeted action to the repo plan; `cancelled` silently swallows a click. Both fail quiet.

## D2 — Pseudo and fallback strips

**Pseudo — Ruling: CONFIRM. Pseudo strip = the existing `{kind:"none"}` repo-level authoring flow, no stamp.**
Rationale: `pseudo.planPath` *is* the legacy root plan, so AI Plan / Import Plan / Open Plan map one-to-one onto the repo-level path and AI Sets uses the module-less decomposition prompt — no new code, no stamp target invented. (The `Assign legacy sets to module…` affordance is a *separate* affordance on `Unassigned`, not one of the four; it does not appear on sole-`Default`.)

**Fallback — Ruling: NO action strip. The existing `undeclared-slug` warning is the remediation surface.**
Rationale: two of four actions are hard-broken (`planPath` is null → Import/Open Plan have no target) and the other two are premature (authoring a plan or decomposing sets for an *undeclared* slug only perpetuates the fallback); the warning already fails loud and the module's sets stay fully visible, so no work is hidden.

- Do **not** ship the full strip (silent no-op buttons = fail-quiet — the worst outcome).
- Deferred, out of the confirmed four-action scope: a single "Declare '<slug>' in modules.yaml" affordance that seeds the S3 appender/`newModule` — call it a follow-up, not S2.

## D3 — Protocol + ARIA + context menu

**Protocol — Ruling: CONFIRM the new `{type:"moduleAction", action, moduleSlug, moduleKind}` message; do NOT route through executeCommand/COMMAND_ALLOWLIST.**
Rationale: a closed action-enum + explicit module identity is self-validating and mirrors `gettingStartedAction`, so the allowlist contract stays untouched; routing via executeCommand would either widen the allowlist or smuggle identity through args.

- Host narrows all three fields (action ∈ enum, kind ∈ {declared,pseudo,fallback}, slug: string); unknown ⇒ drop + log. Dispatch is driven by **authoritative slug resolution**, `moduleKind` is an untrusted cross-check only: `""` ⇒ repo-level (pseudo); resolves ⇒ declared preselect (D1); non-empty-unresolvable ⇒ `unknown-module`. (A message from a fallback row shouldn't exist per D2 — treat as protocol error, drop.)

**ARIA — Ruling: bless the placement (strip = sibling of `.module-header` inside the treeitem, `aria-labelledby` → header only) BUT prescribe the keyboard model: the strip is a `role="toolbar"` SECONDARY TABSTOP, not roving-with-treeitems, not `aria-describedby`.**
Rationale: interactive non-treeitem descendants are tree-safe only if they're excluded from the accessible name *and* kept off the tree's arrow-key roving — a toolbar reached by Tab (Shift+Tab/Esc to exit) with its own internal roving satisfies both, matching the `.row-verification-marker` name-exclusion precedent.

- Buttons `role="button"`, `tabindex=-1` with exactly one `tabindex=0` inside the toolbar (roving via Arrow/Home/End); tree arrow-nav continues to touch treeitems only.
- Toolbar carries its own `aria-label` (e.g. "Module actions"); the treeitem's `aria-labelledby` never references the buttons.

**Context menu — Ruling: NEW `showModuleContextMenu {moduleSlug, moduleKind}` message; do NOT overload `showRowContextMenu`.**
Rationale: module identity and the four-action menu are disjoint from the set-name-keyed row menu — a separate message avoids a polymorphic identity union and keeps each menu's contract single-purpose.

- Host shows an **action-selection** QuickPick (which of the four); the module is already carried, so no module-selection QuickPick and no auto-select notice fire — Amendment 1's "no QuickPick" bans re-picking the *module*, not the context-menu mechanism itself. On pick, dispatch the same handler as `moduleAction`.

## D4 — The spec.md `module:` stamp writer

**(i) Placement + method — Ruling: regex splice inserting `module: <slug>` as the first line immediately after the opening ` ```yaml ` fence of the "Session Set Configuration" block; parse-after guard. No YAML round-trip.**
Rationale: the opening fence is the single deterministic anchor, top-insert touches no existing keys/comments, and a splice + parse-guard preserves formatting where round-trip serialization would destroy comments.

- Existing `module:` line == target ⇒ **no-op** (see iv); != target ⇒ **refuse** (differing stamp on a supposedly-legacy set = stale snapshot, never silently overwrite).
- No config block ⇒ refuse loud. Block present but no ` ```yaml ` fence ⇒ refuse loud (no safe anchor).
- Inserted line's indentation matches the block's key column (col 0 for a plain fenced block).

**(ii) Atomicity — Ruling: two-phase. Phase 1 validate ALL (all-or-nothing gate, nothing written on any predictable refusal). Phase 2 write per-file with parse-after guard, single-file rollback-from-in-memory-original + batch abort on any post-write anomaly. Report successes/skips/failures.**
Rationale: mirrors S3's single-file fail-loud, extended to N files by gating every validation before any byte is written and holding originals (already read for validation) in memory so a corrupt splice rolls back cleanly.

- Phase 1 per set: resolvable spec.md, parseable config block + fence, not stamped-to-a-different-module, target manifest-declared & non-pseudo/non-default. Any failure ⇒ refuse whole batch.
- Phase 2 I/O throw (disk error): stop, report which files were written vs not (unrollable OS failure surfaced loudly) — this is the only partial-state path.

**(iii) Parse-after guard — Ruling: assert (1) the block re-parses as valid YAML, (2) parsed `module` === target slug, (3) exactly one `module:` line in the block, (4) the on-disk file re-read equals the intended spliced content byte-for-byte (diff vs original == the single inserted line, nothing else).**
Rationale: (1)+(2) prove the stamp landed and the block survived; (3)+(4) prove zero collateral mutation — the fail-loud invariant for a format-preserving splice.

**(iv) Guards — Ruling: CONFIRM all.**
Rationale: unstamped *is* the pseudo state, so stamping it back would be semantically wrong, and idempotence must be race-safe.

- Never write `""` / `default` / any pseudo or fallback slug as a target.
- Target slug MUST be manifest-declared, validated against the **current** manifest at write time (not the stale picker snapshot; matches D1's `unknown-module` at the writer gate).
- Set already stamped to the SAME target ⇒ **no-op** (skip, count as already-assigned), never a refusal.
